title: Edgar Morin: 'I doubt humanity even as I believe in it'
subhed: Over a series of conversations held between 2024 and 2026 with Le Monde, the philosopher and WWII resistant analyzed the contemporary world's upheaval, from the rise of authoritarianism in the West to conflicts in the Middle East, as well as the impact of new technologies and environmental issues.

title: Trump cancels envoys' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks

title: 'Everything seemed to be cracking and melting': Former 'liquidator' recounts Chernobyl disaster, 40 years later
subhed: Halyna Kharshenko went to work at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant on April 26, 1986, unaware of the scale and severity of the explosion that had just occurred. The next day, she had to be rushed to a hospital unit where the staff would only approach her in full protective gear.

title: Annette Messager: 'Without art, I would have become a very mean woman'
subhed: 'I wouldn't be where I am today if...' In this series, Le Monde interviews a public figure about a decisive moment in their life.  The internationally renowned visual artist recalls the strange atmosphere of the seaside town of Berck, where she grew up.

title: 'How can anyone love Céline Dion? The hysteria surrounding her Paris concerts is astonishing'
subhed: After the spectacular sale of nearly 500,000 tickets for a series of shows in Paris this autumn, Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin questions in his column what drives people to love (or hate) the singer.

title: In Lebanon, Israel establishes an uninhabited buffer zone behind a 'yellow line'
subhed: The 'demarcation line' drawn by Israel on the third day of the ceasefire has revived memories of the 18-year occupation of Lebanon from 1982 to 2000. The Israeli army has banned Lebanese citizens from entering this 'defense zone,' which covers 6% of the country.
